step_by_step_thinking: \boxed{B} No — The provided medical evidence does not mention isradipine or any calcium channel blocker having a disease-modifying effect on early Parkinson disease. While several articles discuss calcium channel blockers (like nimodipine) in contexts unrelated to PD, none support a role in slowing disease progression. All referenced treatments—levodopa, dopamine agonists (ropinirole, pramipexole), selegiline—are described strictly as symptomatic therapies. Crucially, the literature explicitly states that “prolonged levodopa therapy...does not modify the degeneration process,” reinforcing that current treatments lack disease-modifying properties. Since isradipine is a dihydropyridine calcium channel blocker structurally similar to nimodipine and no evidence supports its use beyond symptom management in PD, we conclude it cannot slow progression of early Parkinson disease.
